Title: Biosynthesis of barley wax β-diketones: a type-III polyketide synthase condensing two fatty acyl units

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: β-diketone formation from plastidial fatty acid synthesis intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: First committed-step enzyme; key enzyme for β-diketone formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes 3-ketoacyl-ACP intermediates from plastidial/type-II fatty acid synthesis to release free 3-ketoacids, mainly C16 3-ketoacid; can also hydrolyze 3-hydroxyacyl-ACP in heterologous assays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Drought resistance, reduced water loss, improved water use efficiency, maintenance of grain yield under stress
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Spike wax; flag leaf sheath wax; upper leaf sheaths, flag leaves and spikes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces the key 3-ketoacid intermediate required for β-diketone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Barley mutant evidence from prior work referenced; heterologous expression in E. coli with metabolite profiling; coupled in vitro enzyme assay with EcACP/EcFabD/MtFabH generating C16 3-ketoacyl-ACP; GC-MS product identification; subcellular localization by GFP in barley protoplasts
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Cloned from flag leaf sheaths and spikes of barley cv. Morex; β-diketone pathway active in upper leaf sheaths, flag leaves and spikes at late vegetative/reproductive stages
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Type-II FAS intermediates; EcACP; EcFabD; MtFabH (assay components)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Late vegetative and reproductive growth stages; drought is associated with β-diketone accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression in yeast did not yield detectable ketoacid products, likely due to incompatibility with type-I FAS or ACP specificity; enzyme tolerated unsaturated substrates in E. coli assays, which may not reflect in planta substrate pools
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: β-diketone biosynthesis may compete with primary lipid metabolism for fatty acid synthesis intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to enhance cereal cuticular β-diketone production and drought tolerance; may need coordination with downstream HvDMP and flux from fatty acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HvDMH is the first committed enzyme in barley β-diketone biosynthesis. 2. It functions as a thioesterase releasing free 3-ketoacids from 3-ketoacyl-ACP intermediates. 3. It preferentially generates mainly C16 3-ketoacid, matching the dominant precursor inferred from barley wax chemistry. 4. HvDMH localizes to plastids/chloroplasts, placing precursor generation in plastidial fatty acid synthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type-III polyketide synthase (PKS); Cys-His-Asn catalytic triad
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control/terminal determinant of direct β-diketone formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes decarboxylative head-to-head condensation of long-chain 3-ketoacids with long-chain fatty acyl-CoAs to directly form β-diketones; does not use malonyl-CoA as extender in this pathway
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: 3-ketoacid (mainly C16, also C14/C18 accepted) and fatty acyl-CoA (preferentially C14/C16)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: β-diketones, mainly C31 14,16-diketone from C16 3-ketoacid + C16 acyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Drought resistance, reduced water loss, improved water use efficiency, maintenance of grain yield under stress
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Spike wax; flag leaf sheath wax; upper leaf sheaths, flag leaves and spikes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly synthesizes β-diketones from 3-ketoacid and acyl-CoA substrates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast feeding assays with exogenous ketoacids/fatty acids; in vitro enzyme assays with purified recombinant protein; deuterium-label feeding; odd/even chain substrate feeding; GC-MS metabolite profiling; subcellular localization by GFP in tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Cloned from flag leaf sheaths of barley cv. Morex; pathway active in β-diketone-accumulating epidermal tissues
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: LACS1 (co-expressed in yeast to enhance substrate uptake)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Late vegetative and reproductive growth stages; drought is associated with β-diketone accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shows unusual PKS chemistry using non-aromatic long-chain acyl-CoA starters and ketoacid extenders; no detectable products with malonyl-CoA extender combinations; substrate preference depends partly on co-substrate chain length, but C16+C16 explains dominant in planta C31 14,16-diketone
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Competes with primary lipid metabolism for plastid-derived fatty acid substrates
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target for increasing protective β-diketone waxes in cereals; should be stacked with HvDMH and supported by precursor supply/transport
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HvDMP is a type-III PKS that directly forms β-diketones by condensing 3-ketoacids with fatty acyl-CoAs. 2. It produced C31 14,16-diketone from C16 3-ketoacid and C16 acyl-CoA in yeast and in vitro. 3. Isotope and odd/even chain feeding demonstrated intact acyl-chain incorporation and head-to-head condensation. 4. HvDMP does not use malonyl-CoA in the tested pathway and localizes to the ER.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely contributes to protective cuticular wax properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic and prior literature context only in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a clustered β-diketone-pathway gene discussed by the paper, but not biochemically characterized here
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential modifier of wax composition, but direct evidence not provided here
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HvDMC is part of the known barley β-diketone gene cluster. 2. In this paper it is proposed, not directly tested, to hydroxylate β-diketones into hydroxy-β-diketones. 3. The core biochemical focus of the Results is on HvDMH and HvDMP rather than HvDMC.
